summary: Session 1 of 2 (Conditional budget block + Build gating): the Full-tier budget block now nests as an indented child of the 'Direct provider API keys' option row and renders only while Full + the api sub-option are selected (omitted, not hidden, matching the block's existing tier-gate pattern; sub-choice flips re-render and gsState preserves the typed value, so an api->copilot->api round-trip restores it). Build is honest about the Copilot seat: client.js validates/posts budget riders only while the block is live, gettingStartedActions drops stray riders under copilot-cli and scopes the Set 063 fail-closed reject to the Direct-API path, and gitScaffold's effectiveBudget caller condition passes no budget to the scaffold step on the Copilot path (writeBudgetYaml unchanged; a missing budget.yaml has documented compat defaults). New Layer-2 suites pin placement/visibility, flip persistence, the Build write matrix at the runScaffold seam, and the action-router matrix; zero semantic edits to pre-existing budget parsing / zero-rule / no-clobber tests (the Set 080 tripwire). tsc clean; Layer-2 1265/0; Layer-3 Playwright 19/19 locally.
verification_notes: Routed gate: REQUIRED (breadth: 8 files >= 4) over the session diff. Cross-provider verification on gpt-5-4 (openai; orchestrator claude/anthropic). Round 1 ISSUES_FOUND with one Major (S081-S1-V1-001): the evidence packet's diff was path-filtered to media/+src/ and omitted the tracked session-state.json Step-1 boundary write visible in git status --short — the L-064-9 evidence-completeness class extended to path-filtered diffs. Remediated by re-sending the complete unfiltered diff; round 2 (substantive narrow re-verify, no max_tier pin) confirmed RESOLVED, the added hunk is workflow ledger state and no other round-1 conclusion changed. Round 2's verdict token was 'RESOLVED' (outside the binary grammar), so round 3 was a wording-only restatement with max_tier pinned per L-064-7: VERIFIED, empty issues list. Structured finding persisted to s1-issues.json with resolution annotations; raw outputs in s1-verification.md / -round-2.md / -round-3.md, never edited.

reason.specifics: Routed ai-assignment recommendation (gemini-pro analysis): Session 2 is procedural UAT-checklist authoring, screenshot, and release mechanics — well-suited to a cheaper orchestrator; no high-end model needed.
